what_it_does: This file is a per-unit mount for the gallery surface. It binds a small 'Ask about this' brain UI to a rendered gallery unit after DNA emits gallery:rendered, using the unit's source address as the brain context handle. It also listens for gallery:direction events, batches them in a microtask, and forwards them to the shared forkBrainCore for route-back writes. Additionally, it binds the same ask affordance to decision cards on decision:rendered.
observations: Assumes window.forkBrainCore is already loaded and throws if missing.
Uses custom DOM events gallery:rendered, gallery:direction, and decision:rendered for wiring.
Creates DOM elements with classes brain-ask, brain-panel, brain-reply, brain-input, and brain-send.
Toggles the brain panel open/closed on repeated clicks of the ask button.
Calls core().talk(replyEl, sourceAddr, question) to perform the brain turn.
Batches direction items via a microtask queue before calling core().writeDirections(items).
Explicitly does NOT listen to gallery:verb, noting that App.tsx's dispatcher owns the decision-card take to avoid double writes.
Exposes window.forkBrainHooks.bindBrain for explicit binding if projection prefers.
Comments enforce an operator-law: UI text must not show the tool name or raw code:// address.
standouts: The deliberate non-consumption of gallery:verb to prevent double-writing the decision take.
The decision:rendered hook that extends the same ask affordance to full-screen decision cards.
Microtask batching of direction events before delegating to the shared core.
Strong 'look vs wire' separation: DNA owns classes/skin, fork owns event wiring.
conventions: Heavy inline comments documenting contracts, ownership, and load order.
Delegates all brain-turn and write logic to a shared global core rather than reimplementing.
concerns: Hard dependency on a global window.forkBrainCore with no local fallback.
_pending batch array is unbounded if flush throws or core is unavailable.
No keyboard handler for Enter in the textarea; only the send button triggers talk.
Event listeners added to dynamically created panel elements are not explicitly removed when the panel is toggled off.
Falls back from d.source to d.address and from address to source, which may mix code:// and run:// handles.
No validation that sourceAddr is a non-empty string before creating UI or calling core().talk.
notes: A thin, event-driven gallery brain binder that stays out of App.tsx's decision-take path and delegates real work to forkBrainCore.
questions: What exact fields does forkBrainCore.talk expect beyond (replyEl, sourceAddr, question)?
What is the full shape of a gallery:direction event detail beyond element_id?
How does DNA guarantee fork-brain-core.js is loaded before this file?
How does DNA emit gallery:rendered and decision:rendered, and what guarantees root is a real Element?
What events or callbacks does core().writeDirections emit on success or failure?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a per-unit mount that binds a brain UI on render and routes direction writes back through the shared core.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be a minimal, reusable wiring layer that adds an interactive brain affordance to rendered units without duplicating brain-turn logic or colliding with App.tsx's decision-take dispatcher.
novelty: Its explicit self-awareness of hook boundaries—documenting which hook owns which event, why a gallery:verb listener was removed, and the load-order contract with the shared core.
contribution: It contributes a per-unit brain UI binding, a decision-card brain binding, and a microtask-batched direction-flushing path that all delegate to the shared brain core, keeping the surface wiring modular and non-overlapping with the App dispatcher.
summary_for_embedding: A gallery-surface IIFE that binds an 'Ask about this' brain panel to rendered units on gallery:rendered and decision:rendered, and batches gallery:direction items for route-back writes via window.forkBrainCore.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a per-unit, non-duplicative brain hook that wires operator questions and direction writes into the shared fork brain core while letting DNA own rendering and App.tsx own decision takes.

symbols.does: Returns the global forkBrainCore or throws if it is not loaded.
symbols.description: A small accessor that enforces the load-order contract: it returns window.forkBrainCore and raises an error if the shared brain core is absent, ensuring the file never runs without its dependency.

symbols.does: Creates and wires an 'Ask about this' brain panel on a rendered unit keyed by an address.
symbols.description: Builds a toggle button and a panel with a reply area, textarea, and send button; guards against rebinding via dataset; and calls core().talk with the unit's source address when the operator sends a question.

symbols.does: Reads the textarea and triggers a brain turn for the bound unit.
symbols.description: A click handler created inside bindBrain that extracts the trimmed question from the panel's textarea and invokes core().talk on the reply container using the unit's source address.

symbols.does: Drains the pending direction batch into the shared core's writeDirections.
symbols.description: Called once per microtask after a gallery:direction event is queued; it clears the pending array and forwards all collected direction items to core().writeDirections for route-back territory writes.
